title: Apple Releases Safari Technology Preview 196 With Bug Fixes and Performance Improvements
content:
Apple today released a new update for Safari Technology Preview, the experimental browser Apple first introduced in March 2016. Apple designed the ŌĆīSafari Technology PreviewŌĆī to test features that may be introduced into future release versions of Safari.


ŌĆīSafari Technology PreviewŌĆī 196 includes fixes and updates for Canvas, CSS, JavaScript, Web API, Web Inspector, WebGL, and WebRTC.

The current ŌĆīSafari Technology PreviewŌĆī release is compatible with machines running macOS Ventura and macOS Sonoma, the latest version of macOS that Apple released in September 2023.

The ŌĆīSafari Technology PreviewŌĆī update is available through the Software Update mechanism in System Preferences or System Settings to anyone who has downloaded the browser. Full release notes for the update are available on the Safari Technology Preview website.

Apple's aim with ŌĆīSafari Technology PreviewŌĆī is to gather feedback from developers and users on its browser development process. ŌĆīSafari Technology PreviewŌĆī can run side-by-side with the existing Safari browser and while designed for developers, it does not require a developer account to download.
Tag: Safari Technology Preview

This article, "Apple Releases Safari Technology Preview 196 With Bug Fixes and Performance Improvements" first appeared on MacRumors.com
